revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=draft|12=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社論社|12=draft|12=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft|12=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Engishiki Jinmyocho|" to "Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|"
revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=draft|12=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=draft|12=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社論社|12=draft|12=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft|12=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=draft|12=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|12=draft|12=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=draft|12=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|12=draft|12=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:玉諸神社 up to rev 92455223
revision.text:
'''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、「玉諸」を社名とする[[神社]]。[[山梨県]]内に二社がある。 {{座標一覧}} == 概要 == 現在二社ある玉諸神社は、いずれも『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「[[甲斐国]][[山梨郡]] 玉諸神社」と記載される[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 * 1.[[玉諸神社#玉諸神社 (甲府市)|玉諸神社]] （[[甲府市]]国玉町） - [[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]、旧[[県社]] * 2.[[玉諸神社#玉諸神社 (甲州市)|玉諸神社]] （[[甲州市]]塩山竹森） - 旧[[郷社]] 二社は祭神・由緒とも完全に異なる神社で、社名は1ではかつての神木「玉室杉」に、2はかつての[[神体]]の水晶玉に由来するとしている。 『式内社調査報告』では、2の周辺では現在も石英の散乱や住居跡も見られることから、玉類の信仰から生まれた神社であり、『[[和名抄]]』に「玉井郷」と記されたことにもつながるとしている。一方、1は古くから「国魂社」として信仰された神社であり、祭神の「國魂神（國玉神）」から社名が生まれたと考えられるが、その「魂」と玉諸の「玉」を結びつけることには無理があるとしている『式内社調査報告』玉諸神社項。。 == 玉諸神社 (甲府市) == {{神社 |名称 = 玉諸神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[甲府市]]国玉町1331 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|國魂大神命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
[[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 国玉社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月15日]]前後の日曜日 |神事 = }} '''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 === 祭神 === * [[大国主|國魂大神命]] 『甲斐国志』では祭神を大己貴命としている。 === 歴史 === [[ファイル:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|御室山]] 社伝では、もとは[[酒折宮]]北方の御室山山上に祀られていたとされる。その後、[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰路において水害防止として現在地に珠を埋め、その上に杉を植え[[依り代]]として国玉大神を祀った。すると洪水は鎮まったことから、のちに社が築かれたとされる。また、この杉は「玉室杉」と呼ばれたことから「玉諸」の名が起こったとされる境内掲示板より。。また、周辺の「国玉（くだま）」の地名は当社に由来している。また「[[甲斐国造]]」系図には、[[塩海足尼]]の子で国造二代の速彦宿禰が「玉緒神」を祭祀したとある[[中田憲信]]「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。。 当社北方の御室山麓に拝殿跡とされる場所があり、拝殿跡が御室山遥拝所の性格を有すること・当社現在地から御室山が望めることから、元々御室山を祀ることに始まったと考えられている『日本の神々』玉諸神社項。。御室山山中には数ヶ所の磐座が残るほか山麓には古墳・遺跡が多く残っていることからもその信仰がうかがわれる。その山頂には山宮が設けられ、かつては神幸が行われていたと伝えられる。 また当社は甲斐国の三宮とされ、春の御幸祭では[[信玄堤]]近くに鎮座する三社神社（甲斐市竜王）・上石田の三社諏訪神社（甲府市上石田）まで一宮（[[笛吹市]]の[[浅間神社 (笛吹市)|浅間神社]]）・二宮（笛吹市の[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]）とともに渡御する川除祭（水防祭）が古くから行われている（現在は竜王三社神社のみ。詳しくは[[信玄堤#竜王河原宿|信玄堤竜王河原宿と御幸祭]]を参照）。御幸祭では「'''ボンボコサン'''」と呼ばれる[[神馬]]が担ぎ出される。これは[[鞍]]の上に[[梵天 (祭具)|梵天]]を立てたもので、江戸時代の[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）作成の三宅済美筆『御幸祭絵図』においても描かれている。また、大正期には[[若尾謹之助]]が「御祭礼及縁日」において記録している。 ただし、古代から中世にかけては由緒以外に史料がなく実態は不明で、竜王三社神社への神幸も実際には[[永禄]]年間（[[1558年]]-[[1570年]]）以降に始まったとされる『日本の神々』一宮浅間神社項。。 [[中世]]には[[武田氏]]から崇敬を受け社殿も造営されたと伝えられるが、[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）の武田氏滅亡時に兵火で焼失した。その後[[徳川氏]]が当地を治めるにあたって保護を受け、[[江戸時代]]には朱印61石が安堵された『山梨県の地名』甲府市 玉諸神社項。。 大正11年、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列した。 === 境内 === ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|本殿 ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|拝殿
平成16年の造営。 ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|神楽殿 * 拝殿跡 （甲府市善光寺3丁目、{{ウィキ座標|35|39|50.56|N|138|35|44.27|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|位置|name=玉諸神社拝殿跡}}） - 当社北方約1.5km、御前塚（ポンポコ塚）近くに所在。御室山の[[遥拝所]]で、[[柴宮神社]]との兼用であったとされる File:The former site of old Tamamoro Shrine Kofu City.JPG|拝殿跡（甲府市善光寺3丁目） === 摂末社 === 境内に数社が鎮座する。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * [[山梨県]][[甲府市]]国玉町（くだまちょう）1331 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[中央本線]] [[酒折駅]] （徒歩約10分） ; 周辺 * [[酒折宮]] * [[国玉の大橋]]（甲斐の大橋）跡 * [[山梨学院大学]] == 玉諸神社 (甲州市) == {{神社 |名称 = 玉諸神社 |画像 = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[甲州市]]塩山竹森3048 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社}} |祭神 = 天羽明玉命 |神体 = （元）水晶玉 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[甲州市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 === 祭神 === * 天羽明玉命 祭神名は「天明玉命」、「[[玉祖命|玉屋命]]」とする文献もある。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。周辺は古くから石英の産出地で、『和名抄』には「玉井郷」の記載も見られることから、玉類の工人集団が居住し、玉類信仰で生まれた神社と考えられている。社伝では、[[神体]]として高さ7尺・周り6尺8寸の水晶の玉に祀ったことから「玉諸」の名が起こったとされる。その水晶は明治初年に盗難に遭い、現存していない。 === 境内 === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|本殿 File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|一の鳥居
鳥居の向うに見える玉宮小学校校舎の裏手に拝殿と本殿がある。 ==== 本宮 ==== * 本殿 * 拝殿 ==== 奥宮 ==== 本宮北東600mの竹森山（水晶山）中腹（{{Coord|35|44|09.70|N|138|45|03.70|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社奥宮}}）に鎮座する。本殿の地下にはかつて水晶の玉があり神体とされていた。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * [[山梨県]][[甲州市]]塩山竹森3048 ; 周辺 * [[竹森のザゼンソウ群]] == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『日本歴史地名大系 山梨県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）甲府市 玉諸神社項、塩山市 玉諸神社項 * 斎藤典男「玉諸神社」（谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海』（[[白水社]]）） * 式内社研究会編纂『式内社調査報告 第10巻』（皇學館大学出版部）玉諸神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまもろしんしや}} [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[category:甲府市の歴史]] [[category:甲州市の歴史]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧県社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:甲斐国|社3]] [[Category:甲府市の建築物]] [[Category:甲州市の建築物]]

'''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、「玉諸」を社名とする[[神社]]。[[山梨県]]内に二社がある。 {{座標一覧}} == 概要 == 現在二社ある玉諸神社は、いずれも『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に「[[甲斐国]][[山梨郡]] 玉諸神社」と記載される[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。 * 1.[[玉諸神社#玉諸神社 (甲府市)|玉諸神社]] （[[甲府市]]国玉町） - [[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]、旧[[県社]] * 2.[[玉諸神社#玉諸神社 (甲州市)|玉諸神社]] （[[甲州市]]塩山竹森） - 旧[[郷社]] 二社は祭神・由緒とも完全に異なる神社で、社名は1ではかつての神木「玉室杉」に、2はかつての[[神体]]の水晶玉に由来するとしている。 『式内社調査報告』では、2の周辺では現在も石英の散乱や住居跡も見られることから、玉類の信仰から生まれた神社であり、『[[和名抄]]』に「玉井郷」と記されたことにもつながるとしている。一方、1は古くから「国魂社」として信仰された神社であり、祭神の「國魂神（國玉神）」から社名が生まれたと考えられるが、その「魂」と玉諸の「玉」を結びつけることには無理があるとしている『式内社調査報告』玉諸神社項。。 == 玉諸神社 (甲府市) == {{神社 |名称 = 玉諸神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
鳥居 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[甲府市]]国玉町1331 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社}} |祭神 = [[大国主|國魂大神命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
[[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 国玉社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月15日]]前後の日曜日 |神事 = }} '''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[甲斐国]][[一宮|三宮]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 === 祭神 === * [[大国主|國魂大神命]] 『甲斐国志』では祭神を大己貴命としている。 === 歴史 === [[ファイル:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|御室山]] 社伝では、もとは[[酒折宮]]北方の御室山山上に祀られていたとされる。その後、[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰路において水害防止として現在地に珠を埋め、その上に杉を植え[[依り代]]として国玉大神を祀った。すると洪水は鎮まったことから、のちに社が築かれたとされる。また、この杉は「玉室杉」と呼ばれたことから「玉諸」の名が起こったとされる境内掲示板より。。また、周辺の「国玉（くだま）」の地名は当社に由来している。また「[[甲斐国造]]」系図には、[[塩海足尼]]の子で国造二代の速彦宿禰が「玉緒神」を祭祀したとある[[中田憲信]]「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。。 当社北方の御室山麓に拝殿跡とされる場所があり、拝殿跡が御室山遥拝所の性格を有すること・当社現在地から御室山が望めることから、元々御室山を祀ることに始まったと考えられている『日本の神々』玉諸神社項。。御室山山中には数ヶ所の磐座が残るほか山麓には古墳・遺跡が多く残っていることからもその信仰がうかがわれる。その山頂には山宮が設けられ、かつては神幸が行われていたと伝えられる。 また当社は甲斐国の三宮とされ、春の御幸祭では[[信玄堤]]近くに鎮座する三社神社（甲斐市竜王）・上石田の三社諏訪神社（甲府市上石田）まで一宮（[[笛吹市]]の[[浅間神社 (笛吹市)|浅間神社]]）・二宮（笛吹市の[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]）とともに渡御する川除祭（水防祭）が古くから行われている（現在は竜王三社神社のみ。詳しくは[[信玄堤#竜王河原宿|信玄堤竜王河原宿と御幸祭]]を参照）。御幸祭では「'''ボンボコサン'''」と呼ばれる[[神馬]]が担ぎ出される。これは[[鞍]]の上に[[梵天 (祭具)|梵天]]を立てたもので、江戸時代の[[宝暦]]9年（[[1759年]]）作成の三宅済美筆『御幸祭絵図』においても描かれている。また、大正期には[[若尾謹之助]]が「御祭礼及縁日」において記録している。 ただし、古代から中世にかけては由緒以外に史料がなく実態は不明で、竜王三社神社への神幸も実際には[[永禄]]年間（[[1558年]]-[[1570年]]）以降に始まったとされる『日本の神々』一宮浅間神社項。。 [[中世]]には[[武田氏]]から崇敬を受け社殿も造営されたと伝えられるが、[[天正]]10年（[[1582年]]）の武田氏滅亡時に兵火で焼失した。その後[[徳川氏]]が当地を治めるにあたって保護を受け、[[江戸時代]]には朱印61石が安堵された『山梨県の地名』甲府市 玉諸神社項。。 大正11年、[[近代社格制度]]において[[県社]]に列した。 === 境内 === ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|本殿 ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|拝殿
平成16年の造営。 ファイル:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|神楽殿 * 拝殿跡 （甲府市善光寺3丁目、{{ウィキ座標|35|39|50.56|N|138|35|44.27|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|位置|name=玉諸神社拝殿跡}}） - 当社北方約1.5km、御前塚（ポンポコ塚）近くに所在。御室山の[[遥拝所]]で、[[柴宮神社]]との兼用であったとされる File:The former site of old Tamamoro Shrine Kofu City.JPG|拝殿跡（甲府市善光寺3丁目） === 摂末社 === 境内に数社が鎮座する。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * [[山梨県]][[甲府市]]国玉町（くだまちょう）1331 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[中央本線]] [[酒折駅]] （徒歩約10分） ; 周辺 * [[酒折宮]] * [[国玉の大橋]]（甲斐の大橋）跡 * [[山梨学院大学]] == 玉諸神社 (甲州市) == {{神社 |名称 = 玉諸神社 |画像 = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[甲州市]]塩山竹森3048 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社}} |祭神 = 天羽明玉命 |神体 = （元）水晶玉 |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）[[論社]]
旧[[郷社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''玉諸神社'''（たまもろじんじゃ）は、[[山梨県]][[甲州市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]][[論社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 === 祭神 === * 天羽明玉命 祭神名は「天明玉命」、「[[玉祖命|玉屋命]]」とする文献もある。 === 歴史 === 創建は不詳。周辺は古くから石英の産出地で、『和名抄』には「玉井郷」の記載も見られることから、玉類の工人集団が居住し、玉類信仰で生まれた神社と考えられている。社伝では、[[神体]]として高さ7尺・周り6尺8寸の水晶の玉に祀ったことから「玉諸」の名が起こったとされる。その水晶は明治初年に盗難に遭い、現存していない。 === 境内 === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|本殿 File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|一の鳥居
鳥居の向うに見える玉宮小学校校舎の裏手に拝殿と本殿がある。 ==== 本宮 ==== * 本殿 * 拝殿 ==== 奥宮 ==== 本宮北東600mの竹森山（水晶山）中腹（{{Coord|35|44|09.70|N|138|45|03.70|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=玉諸神社奥宮}}）に鎮座する。本殿の地下にはかつて水晶の玉があり神体とされていた。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * [[山梨県]][[甲州市]]塩山竹森3048 ; 周辺 * [[竹森のザゼンソウ群]] == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『日本歴史地名大系 山梨県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）甲府市 玉諸神社項、塩山市 玉諸神社項 * 斎藤典男「玉諸神社」（谷川健一 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海』（[[白水社]]）） * 式内社研究会編纂『式内社調査報告 第10巻』（皇學館大学出版部）玉諸神社項 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまもろしんしや}} [[Category:山梨県の神社]] [[category:甲府市の歴史]] [[category:甲州市の歴史]] [[Category:甲斐国の式内社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧県社]] [[Category:山梨県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三宮 (社格)]] [[Category:甲斐国|社3]] [[Category:甲府市の建築物]] [[Category:甲州市の建築物]]

revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] [[Category:酒折宮]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]

revision.text:
'''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refers to a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the {{ill|third-Ranked shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinai-sha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinai-sha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 鳥居.JPG|280px]]
Torii gate | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|39|10.35|N|138|35|54.19|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = [[Okuninushi|Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto]] | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[April 15]] }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinai-sha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and as the {{ill|third shrine|ja|三宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kai Province|ja|甲斐国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Okuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the {{ill|Tokugawa clan|ja|徳川氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * {{ill|Yamanashi Gakuin University|ja|山梨学院大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = {{ill|Koshu|ja|甲州市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = [[Shikinai-sha]] (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "{{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinai-sha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kai Province Shikinai-sha]] [[Category:Kofu]] [[Category:Koshu]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōfu City]] [[Category:History of Kōshū City]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Kōfu Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Third Shrines (type)]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōfu City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Kōshū City]] }} [[sn:Tamamoro Shrine]] [[ja:玉諸神社]]
