Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:玉諸神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11572792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}} refers to a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District, Kai Province|1=Yamanashi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469930}}, [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third-Ranked shrine]] of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]

According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[ja:玉諸神社]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11572792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}} refers to a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District, Kai Province|1=Yamanashi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469930}}, [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third-Ranked shrine]] of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[ja:玉諸神社]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}} refers to a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District, Kai Province|1=Yamanashi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469930}}, [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third-Ranked shrine]] of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:玉諸神社]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11572792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (玉諸神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines with this name located in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} == Overview == {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine|ja|玉諸神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine|qq=}} refers to a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] with the name "Tamamoro." There are two shrines by this name in [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. {{GeoGroup}} The two existing Tamamoro Shrines are both considered {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} candidates mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as "Tamamoro Shrine, {{ill|Yamanashi District, Kai Province|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District, Kai Province|1=Yamanashi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469930}}, [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]." * 1. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲府市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], Kokudama Town) – Recognized as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third-Ranked shrine]] of Kai Province; former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. * 2. {{ill|Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja|玉諸神社 (甲州市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 玉諸神社|1=Tamamoro Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572792}} ([[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], Shioyama Takemori) – Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. These two shrines have completely different enshrined deities and origins. The name "Tamamoro" at the Kofu shrine originates from the former sacred tree "Tamamuro Cedar," while at the Koshu shrine, it derives from the shrine’s former sacred object, a crystal sphere. According to the *Shikinaisha Research Report*, the Koshu shrine was founded on an ancient belief in sacred stones, as scattered quartz fragments and residential remains are still visible in the area. This also connects to the listing of "Tamai District" in the {{ill|Wamyosho|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyosho|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄|1=Wamyō Ruijushō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2702542}}. On the other hand, the Kofu shrine was historically venerated as a "Kokudama Shrine," deriving its name from the enshrined deity "Kunidama no Kami" (國魂神). However, the connection between the "tama" (玉) in Tamamoro and the "tama" in Kunidama is considered tenuous.*Shikinaisha Research Report*, Tamamoro Shrine section. == Tamamoro Shrine (Kofu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|35.652875|N|138.598386|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
[[Kai Province]] [[Third Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | established = Unknown | festival = Sunday around [[:en:April 15|April 15]] | prefecture= {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720}} }} '''Tamamoro Shrine''' (たまもろじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is recognized as a {{ill|Shikinaisha candidate|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft論社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha candidate|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and as the [[:en:Sannomiya|third shrine]] of [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]]. Its former status was a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. === Enshrined deity === * [[:en:Ōkuninushi|Okuninushi]] (Referred to as Kunidama Ōkami-no-Mikoto) The *Kai Kokushi* identifies the enshrined deity as Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto (大己貴命). === History === [[File:御室山 (甲府市).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Mt. Omuro]] According to shrine tradition, the deity was originally enshrined on Mount Omuro, north of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}. Later, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} passed through the region on his eastern expedition, he buried a sacred jewel at the present site, planted a cedar tree over it as a sacred object, and enshrined Kunidama Ōkami to prevent flooding. The floodwaters receded, and a shrine was eventually built there. The sacred tree, known as the "Tamamuro Cedar," is said to have given rise to the name "Tamamoro."From the shrine’s information board. The surrounding area of Kokudama (くだま) is also named after the shrine. The genealogy of the {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kai Kokuzo|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokuzo|qq=}} records that the second-generation chief, Hayahiko-no-Sukune (速彦宿禰), enshrined "Tamano-o-Kami."{{ill|Norinobu Nakata|ja|中田憲信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Norinobu Nakata|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491601}}, "Kai Kokuzo," *Shokeifu* Vol. 7. The former site of the shrine’s main hall, located at the foot of Mount Omuro, is considered a relic of an ancient {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}}. Since Mount Omuro is still visible from the current location, it is believed that the shrine originated as a place of worship for the mountain.*Japanese Deities* (日本の神々), Tamamoro Shrine section. Several sacred rock formations (iwakura) remain on the mountain, and numerous ancient tombs and archaeological sites dot the area. A yamamiya (mountain shrine) once existed at the summit, and there are accounts of ancient processions ascending the mountain. During the Edo period, the shrine received patronage from the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa clan]], and its lands were confirmed with a stipend of 61 koku.*Geographical Names of Yamanashi Prefecture* (山梨県の地名), Kofu City, Tamamoro Shrine section. In 1922, the shrine was elevated to Prefectural Shrine status under the modern shrine Ranking system. === Grounds === File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 本殿.JPG|Main Hall File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 拝殿.JPG|Worship Hall
Reconstructed in 2004. File:玉諸神社 (甲府市) 神楽殿.JPG|Kagura Hall === Surroundings === * {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}} * Ruins of {{ill|Kokudama Bridge|ja|国玉の大橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokudama Bridge|1=Kokudama Bridge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421176}} (Kai's Great Bridge) * [[:en:Yamanashi Gakuin University|Yamanashi Gakuin University]] == Tamamoro Shrine (Koshu) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tamamoro Shrine | image = [[File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City, Oratory.JPG|280px]]
Worship Hall | location = [[:en:Kōshū, Yamanashi|Koshu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|35|44|0.5|N|138|44|41.6|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark}} | deity = Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto | sacred object = (Formerly) a crystal sphere | former status = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Minor) [[candidate shrine]]
Former [[:en:Gosha|Gosha]] | established = Unknown }} === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja|天羽明玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama-no-Hakarudama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Some sources list the deity as "Ama-no-Akarutama-no-Mikoto" or "[[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]." === History === The shrine’s founding date is unknown. The area has long been known for quartz production, and historical records in the *Wamyosho* mention "Tamai District." It is believed that the shrine originated from gemstone worship. The original sacred object was a massive crystal sphere, but it was stolen in the early Meiji period. === Grounds === File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Main hall.JPG|Main Hall File:Tamamoro Shrine Koshu City,Torii in the first.JPG|First Torii Gate == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * ''Yamanashi Prefecture Place names'' (Heibonsha), entries on Kōfu City and Shiozawa City, Tamamoro Shrine * Saitō Norio, "Tamamoro Shrine," in Ken’ichi Tanigawa, ed., ''Japanese Deities - Shrines and Sacred Sites,'' vol. 10, Tokai (Hakusuisha) * ''Shikinaisha Research Report,'' vol. 10, Tamamoro Shrine entry (Kōgakkan University Press) {{translated page|ja|玉諸神社|version=92455223|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:玉諸神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11572792}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kōfu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Buildings in Koshu]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:History of Koshu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Kai Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine rank of Sannomiya]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
